---
title: "01 — Exploratory Data Analysis"
author: "PSTAT 134 — AI Writing Detector"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    embed-resources: true
    theme: simplex
    code-fold: show
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# Make src/ importable when this notebook is rendered from the notebooks/ folder.
PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "notebooks" else Path.cwd()
sys.path.append(str(PROJECT_ROOT))

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns

from src.data import load_essays
from src.features import stylometric_features

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
```

## Load the data

```{python}
#| label: load
df = load_essays()
df.shape
```

```{python}
df.head()
```

## Class balance

The dataset is intentionally balanced — confirm the 50/50 split is preserved.

```{python}
#| label: class-balance
df["generated"].value_counts().rename({0: "human", 1: "ai"})
```

## Length distributions

```{python}
#| label: lengths
df = df.assign(
    n_chars=df["text"].str.len(),
    n_words=df["text"].str.split().map(len),
)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
sns.histplot(
    data=df, x="n_words", hue="generated", bins=50, kde=True, ax=axes[0]
)
axes[0].set_title("Word count by class")
sns.boxplot(data=df, x="generated", y="n_words", ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title("Word count, boxplot")
plt.tight_layout()
```

## Stylometric features

```{python}
#| label: stylometric
style = stylometric_features(df["text"])
style["generated"] = df["generated"].values
style.groupby("generated").mean(numeric_only=True).T
```

```{python}
#| label: stylometric-plot
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4))
for ax, col in zip(axes, ["avg_sentence_len_words", "type_token_ratio", "punct_rate"]):
    sns.boxplot(data=style, x="generated", y=col, ax=ax)
    ax.set_title(col)
plt.tight_layout()
```

## Notes for the team

- Are the two classes separable on simple length / punctuation features alone?
- Which features look most promising as descriptive variables for the report?
- Anything weird (extremely short essays, duplicated text, encoding issues) we should clean before modeling?
